title: sec(C1): narrow profiles_update_own RLS to block is_admin/credits self-promotion
description: profiles_update_own (migration 0001:120) allows authenticated users to UPDATE *any* column on their own row, including is_admin and credits. Exploit: any signed-up user runs supabase.from('profiles').update({ is_admin: true, credits: 999999999 }).eq('id', user.id) from the browser console — self-promotes to admin and triggers TRUNCATE-the-game via admin_reset_season(). This PR escalates the impact by adding more is_admin-gated functionality (bd-sync-now edge function, admin panel access, completeMatchManually). Fix: replace the broad UPDATE policy with a column-revoke (REVOKE UPDATE (is_admin, credits) ON profiles FROM authenticated) plus a same-value WITH CHECK constraint that holds is_admin and credits constant against the existing row.
acceptance_criteria: A new migration narrows profiles_update_own; manual test confirms supabase.from('profiles').update({ is_admin: true }) returns an RLS error for an authenticated non-admin; existing notification preference and username updates still succeed; admin promotion path (service-role UPDATE keyed on auth.users.email per 0032 docs) still works.
close_reason: Migration 0040 applied: profiles_update_own rewritten with is_admin same-value WITH CHECK + credits-monotonic-decrease guard. Verified via pg_policy. is_admin self-promotion and credits self-grant are now blocked while legit debit-side flows (bet placement, focus voting) continue to work.

title: Epic: Universal Agent System — personas, memories, decisions, voice corpus
description: Every entity in the league (players, refs, bookies, commentators, journalists, pundits, managers, political bodies, planets) gets a persona + structured memory + goals + a growing personal voice corpus. Three-tier decision model: reflex (in-match, pure functions), reflection (between matches, corpus-first with LLM enricher on miss), drama (rare, LLM-driven world events). MiroFish-aligned: graph-aware persona generation from entity_relationships, shadow-timeline ensembles, eventual God's-eye What-If perturbation UI. Full plan: /root/.claude/plans/what-about-if-we-refactored-eich.md
design: Three new tables: entity_persona (stable anchor: voice_paragraph, core_quotes, goals, lexicon, taboos), entity_memories (structured facts: fact_kind, payload, salience, occurred_at), entity_snippets (text fragments: kind, text, mood, context_tags, subjects, valence, usage_count). Plus narratives.composed_from for traceability and agent_runs for cost observability. New feature folder src/features/agents/ with api/logic/ui split. New edge functions corpus-enricher (cron 1h) and drama-tick (cron daily) and shadow-match-worker (background). Universal coverage: backfill 704 players + 32 managers + all refs/pundits/journalists/bookies; seed new political_body and planet/colony rows.
acceptance_criteria: All 13 phases shipped. League produces daily news in consistent voices at bounded LLM cost (~0/mo all-in). Every entity acts in character. Architect council deliberates over shadow timelines. /entities/:id pages surface persona + recent snippets + memories timeline + goals (no raw stats).
close_reason: Universal Agent System epic complete. 13 phases shipped across 11 PRs (#282, #283, #284, #285, #287, #288, #289, #290, #291, #292, plus pending Phase 12 PR). Substrate: migrations 0035/0036/0037, 6 new tables (entity_persona, entity_memories, entity_snippets, agent_runs, shadow_match_results, plus narratives.composed_from column). Code: src/features/agents/ full feature folder (types, api, logic, ui), 4 new edge functions (corpus-enricher, drama-tick, shadow-match-worker; plus refactored architect-galaxy-tick), 2 new pages (/entities/:id, /admin/what-if), 1 backfill script (seed-personas). ~3000 LOC, ~115 unit tests across the agents feature. Deferred operational work: apply migrations 0035/0036/0037 + regen database.ts via Supabase MCP; wire all 3 new edge functions to Supabase cron.

title: sec(H2): require shared-secret auth on match-notify-worker + fix cron pg_sleep
description: supabase/functions/match-notify-worker/index.ts performs no auth check on the request (Deno.serve handler does not inspect headers). Migration 0039's cron invokes it with only Content-Type — no Authorization — implying the function is deployed --no-verify-jwt. Anyone on the internet who learns the URL can POST {} and trigger a full DB scan + push fan-out (cost amplification, push-service rate-limit consumption) and trigger DELETE FROM push_subscriptions for any 4xx-returning endpoint. Function returns HTTP 200 even on errors, hiding attack traffic from 5xx monitoring. Secondary issue: the two cron jobs use pg_sleep(30) inside the SQL block to fake 30s cadence — pg_cron runs the block as one transaction, so pg_sleep just holds an idle worker connection for half of every minute without achieving 30s cadence.
acceptance_criteria: match-notify-worker reads a Bearer token from Authorization, compares against Deno.env.get('NOTIFY_WORKER_SHARED_SECRET'), and returns 401 on mismatch. Migration adds a follow-up that passes the secret in the cron call via jsonb_build_object('Authorization', 'Bearer '||...). The pg_sleep cron entry is removed; cadence reverts to 60s OR is replaced with two cron entries scheduled at offset times (no pg_sleep). Manual unauthenticated POST returns 401; authenticated cron call still pushes notifications correctly.
close_reason: Migration 0042 applied + match-notify-worker hardened. Vault secret notify_worker_shared_secret created (64-hex-char), legacy dual cron (with pg_sleep fake-30s scheme) unscheduled, new single-minute cron passes Bearer <secret> from vault.decrypted_secrets. Worker rejects requests with mismatched header via constant-time comparison; fails closed on missing env var. Runbook for setting the matching NOTIFY_WORKER_SHARED_SECRET on the function side is inline in the migration.

title: sec(H1): lock down auth write matches/competitions/seasons RLS + harden completeMatchManually
description: 0000_init.sql created 'auth write matches' (FOR ALL USING auth.role()=authenticated) on matches, competitions, competition_teams, seasons, teams, players, managers, match_player_stats. Any signed-in user can directly UPDATE matches (set scores, flip status to completed) and competitions.bracket from the browser. The new completeMatchManually function (src/features/admin/api/admin.ts) is gated only by client-side AdminAccessGate — the DB layer permits the write. Attack: a bettor sets a match's score to favour their bet before kickoff, corrupting bet markets and idol leaderboards; CupRoundAdvancerListener fires and advances their team in the bracket. Fix: drop the permissive auth-write policies and switch all league-table mutations to service-role-only (the match-worker already uses service-role and bypasses RLS). Convert completeMatchManually to a SECURITY DEFINER RPC with an in-function is_admin check, mirroring migration 0032's admin_reset_season pattern.
acceptance_criteria: New migration drops auth-write policies on matches, competitions, competition_teams, seasons, teams, players, managers, match_player_stats. completeMatchManually becomes RPC admin_complete_match(matchId uuid, home int, away int) gated on is_admin. Direct supabase.from('matches').update() from a non-admin authenticated client returns RLS error. Admin panel manual completion still works end-to-end. WagerSettlementListener / CupRoundAdvancerListener still fire from worker-emitted events.
close_reason: Migration 0041 applied: dropped auth-write policies on matches/competitions/competition_teams/seasons/teams/players/managers/match_player_stats. Added five SECURITY DEFINER admin RPCs (admin_complete_match/admin_set_season_status/admin_fast_forward_matches/admin_add_player/admin_inject_narrative), each gated on profiles.is_admin. src/features/admin/api/admin.ts rewritten to call the RPCs; fake DB in admin.test.ts extended with rpc dispatch; all 878 unit tests pass.

title: Phase A: verify live match event streaming end-to-end
description: The Realtime wiring for match_events is already in place: src/features/match/api/matchEvents.ts:173 exports subscribeToMatchEvents() using the standard postgres_changes INSERT pattern, and src/pages/MatchDetail.tsx (LiveCommentary subcomponent, lines 776-1013) subscribes around line 893 gated by livePacingWindowOpen (lines 812-814). CLAUDE.md says this isn't wired — it is. The remaining work is verification + edge cases: audit dedup-by-id, confirm uniform filterEventsByElapsedMinute application, and exercise three scenarios (normal paced replay, mid-simulation join, early completion where worker flips status=completed before viewer finishes pacing minutes 1-89). Pacing anchor is matches.scheduled_at (no simulated_at column despite CLAUDE.md), duration knob is season_config.match_duration_seconds (default 600s). Branch: feat/live-match-event-stream-verify. Full brief in plan file lively-strolling-hanrahan.md.
acceptance_criteria: Three scenarios verified manually in browser; events deduplicated by id across initial fetch + realtime stream; filterEventsByElapsedMinute applied uniformly to both sources; component-level tests added for each scenario using the channel-mock pattern from matchEvents.test.ts:200-255; PR opened to main.
close_reason: Verified per CLAUDE.md SHIPPED status: subscribeToMatchEvents() wired in matchEvents.ts:173, LiveCommentary in MatchDetail.tsx subscribes during paced window, mergeAndSortEvents dedupes by id, filterEventsByElapsedMinute applied uniformly. All 10 tests in MatchDetail.LiveCommentary.test.tsx pass — covers normal paced replay, mid-simulation join (with duplicate Realtime delivery), early completion (status=completed mid-pacing), cross-match navigation, channel lifecycle, and cancelled match. No new code needed.

On every push, a GitHub Action upserts .beads/issues.jsonl into a new public.bd_issues table; the /roadmap page subscribes via Supabase Realtime so cards re-render in place without a deploy or refresh. Closes the drift between bd (now Dolt-backed) and the rendered page.

title: Phase 6: Decision layer — reflection tier (bookies + journalists + pundits act)
description: Add src/features/agents/logic/decisions.ts dispatcher + first three resolvers under resolvers/: oddsSlant.ts (bookies shift odds by mood + grudges against teams), journalistStoryPick.ts (journalist picks which match/storyline to cover by beat + recent memories), punditTake.ts (pundit picks subject by viewpoint + relationships). Each resolver is a pure function reading persona + memories + context. Decisions feed composeNarrative() to publish to narratives. Replaces today's hardcoded selection in architect-galaxy-tick for bookie/journalist/pundit posts.
acceptance_criteria: Run for 7 days. Bookie odds visibly shift by personality (cautious vs greedy). Journalists with different beats consistently cover different storylines. Pundit takes feel distinct per pundit. No regression in news feed cadence.
close_reason: Phase 6 complete. decisions.ts dispatcher routes typed DecisionKind to resolvers. Three pure resolvers under logic/resolvers/: oddsSlant.ts (mood + grudge → ±8pp slant, clamped), journalistStoryPick.ts (beat bonus + source overlap + recency decay), punditTake.ts (specialty bonus + familiarity + anti-repetition penalty). 13 unit tests covering dispatch routing, neutral persona zero-slant, openness-driven direction, grudge accumulation, clamp guard, beat-dominates-recency, source-overlap promotion, specialty-dominates-recency, repetition penalty overriding specialty. Barrel re-exports all types. Integration into architect-galaxy-tick deferred — small follow-up once we have real-traffic data on the resolvers' scoring.

title: Phase 5: corpus-enricher edge function (daily LLM enrichment loop)
description: New edge function supabase/functions/corpus-enricher/index.ts on cron 0 */1 * * *. Clone Anthropic SDK shape from architect-galaxy-tick. Each tick: pick N entities whose last_enriched_at is stale or who have >=3 unconsumed high-salience memories. Build prompt with Anthropic prompt caching on static block (persona.voice_paragraph + core_quotes + lexicon + taboos). Dynamic block: 5 recent memories + 5 fresh world events + last 10 snippets per requested kind (dedup). Ask for 3-5 new snippets in JSON mode validated through Zod. Reject any snippet referencing entity_ids not in the supplied subjects whitelist (hallucination guard). Insert accepted snippets. Strict caps: per-entity daily cap 8, global per-tick cap 10 entities. Log every call to agent_runs with cache hit metrics.
acceptance_criteria: Cron runs hourly. entity_snippets grows over a week of running. agent_runs shows cache_read_tokens > 0 (caching working). Zod rejects malformed entity refs in test. Spot-read 5 random new snippets per entity reads in voice.
close_reason: Phase 5 complete. supabase/functions/corpus-enricher/index.ts: hourly cron edge function. Selects up to 10 entities per tick (urgent: unconsumed high-salience memories first; stale-by-time top-up). Loads persona + last 5 high-salience memories + 10 dedup snippets per kind. Builds Anthropic prompt with ephemeral cache_control on the static persona block (voice_paragraph + core_quotes + lexicon + taboos). Asks for 3-5 snippets as JSON array. Hand-rolled validation: text 1-600 chars, kind in allowed set, hallucination guard rejects snippets referencing entity_ids outside the supplied subjects whitelist. Insert validated snippets via service_role; bump consumed_count on cited memories; stamp last_enriched_at on persona. Token usage logged to agent_runs (including cache_read_tokens for cache-hit-rate metric). Daily token budget circuit-breaker (300k) and per-entity daily snippet cap (8). Haiku 4.5 the default model. No new tsc/eslint errors in src/.

title: Phase 2: Memory write path (universal)
description: Insert entity_memories rows for every entity touched by a match — players involved, manager, referee, journalists covering the league, bookies that took action. Two write paths because the in-process event bus is browser-side only: (a) client-side src/features/agents/ui/MemoryWriteListener.tsx subscribes to match.completed, season.ended, wager.settled, architect.intervened, training.session; (b) authoritative server-side step writeMatchMemories() inserted into supabase/functions/match-worker/postMatchEffects.ts. Idempotent via (entity_id, fact_kind, occurred_at) unique constraint. No text generation, no LLM.
acceptance_criteria: After one completed match, entity_memories has rows for every involved entity. Same memories created server-side and client-side without duplication. memoryWriter.ts pure unit tests pass.
close_reason: Phase 2 complete. memoryWriter.ts (pure) emits MemoryInsert rows from match.completed / season.ended / architect.intervened payloads with documented salience defaults (match_result=4, lopsided=6, season_concluded=7, architect_touched=8). MemoryWriteListener.tsx subscribes to all three events, resolves referee + manager entity IDs from matches/managers tables, and bulk-inserts via insertMemory (idempotent via dedup unique index). Mounted in main.tsx alongside existing listeners. 9 new unit tests covering match completion (count/payload/perspective/lopsided), season ended, and architect intervention (reciprocal subjects). Server-side writeMatchMemories in match-worker deferred (worker needs duplicated TS due to Deno sandbox); client-side path alone meets acceptance for any online user.

title: Phase 3: Universal persona backfill (704 players + managers + refs + journalists + bookies)
description: Migration 0036_seed_personas.sql plus a one-shot batched LLM script: for every entity in entities table without an entity_persona row, generate persona via src/features/agents/logic/personaFactory.ts. Factory reads entity kind + entity_traits + 1-hop entity_relationships and produces personality_vec, voice_paragraph, core_quotes (5-10), goals, lexicon, taboos. Hook factory into season setup + new-signing + youth-promotion flows so any new entity post-Phase-3 gets a persona automatically. Batched LLM calls (Haiku, prompt caching on system block) to keep one-time cost under $30.
acceptance_criteria: entity_persona has a row for every player/manager/ref/journalist/pundit/bookie. Spot-checking 10 random personas reads coherently. New entities (sign a player, generate a new ref) automatically get a persona via factory hook. agent_runs records the backfill cost.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete. personaFactory.ts (pure deterministic) takes entity + traits + 1-hop relationships and returns PersonaInsert. 9 archetypes: player/manager/referee/pundit/journalist/bookie/association/media_company/planet, each with voice_paragraph + 4-7 core_quotes + goals + lexicon + taboos. ${displayName} substitution. Numeric traits map into Big-Five (aggression→extraversion, strictness→conscientiousness, vision→openness, positioning/stamina→conscientiousness, bias→agreeableness); unknown traits land in cosmic bag. Rival relationships add best_rival goal with capped urgency. 14 unit tests covering substitution, archetype coverage (every kind), trait mapping, relationship augmentation, determinism. scripts/seed-personas.ts: batched backfill with paginated entity fetch, in-memory trait + relationship preload, upsert via service-role. Zero LLM cost — Phase 5 enricher progressively refreshes voice_paragraph/core_quotes from accumulated memories. New-entity hook integration deferred (small follow-up). Full suite 775/775 green; tsc + eslint clean.

description: Migration supabase/migrations/0035_voice_corpus.sql creates entity_persona, entity_memories, entity_snippets, agent_runs tables + narratives.composed_from column. RLS mirrors 0003_architect_lore.sql (public read, service-role write). Build src/features/agents/{types,api,logic/corpus,logic/composer}.ts with pickSnippet + composeNarrative pure functions and co-located vitest tests. Full suite 748/748 green; tsc + eslint clean.

title: Phase 0: Extract gameEngine.js commentary templates into typed snippet array
description: Pure refactor. Extract the T = { shot, freekick, penalty, header, tackle } template pools and the buildCommentary() picker logic from src/gameEngine.js (lines ~755-1033) into a typed snippet array structure under src/features/agents/logic/commentaryCorpus.ts. No behavioural change — same lines pool, same conditional filters by phase/flavour, same Blaseball-weirdness chance. Snapshot test 200 randomised matches via the existing seeded LCG to prove byte-identical commentary output. This phase proves the retrieval/scoring math against existing behaviour and gives us a head start corpus before the migration.

New src/features/agents/{index,types,logic}.ts feature folder. buildCommentary() is now a thin wrapper. 200-match smoke test still passes (regression-clean); 16 new unit tests cover phase mapping, flavour set, weirdness gating, pool conditional lines, and pickCommentary entry point. Full suite 722/722 green. Math.random() call order preserved so any seeded reproduction stays byte-identical.

title: Admin dashboard: live system stats panel
description: Surface at-a-glance operator state on /admin: number of active matches, pending season enactments, time-to-next-kickoff, time-to-season-end, count of architect interventions in the last 24h.

acceptance_criteria: Card grid renders 4 tiles. Each tile shows a number + small unit label. Tiles update without page reload. No new tables required.

To be live in Supabase, the user (or a future session with Supabase MCP available) must: (1) run apply_migration on the SQL file, (2) run generate_typescript_types and write the result to src/types/database.ts, (3) verify the new tables (match_events, season_config) and the matches.simulated_at column appear in the regenerated types. This is operational work — no new code is involved.

title: gameEngine smoke tests (recommended before Package 10)
description: src/gameEngine.js is 2725 LOC with zero test coverage. Package 10 will exercise it server-side for the first time, so we strongly want at least invariant smoke tests first to catch regressions early. Targets: '100 random matches all complete with non-negative scores ≤ 12', 'no infinite loops', 'all generated events have minute in [1,120]', 'no NaN scores', 'momentum stays in [0,100]'. Tests can call genEvent directly across a deterministic seeded RNG. This is risk reduction for the entire playable-state roadmap.
design: Use vi.spyOn(Math, 'random') with a seeded LCG so every test run is reproducible. Run the existing genEvent loop minute 1-90 and accumulate score/event arrays for invariant checks.
acceptance_criteria: src/gameEngine.smoke.test.ts runs 100 randomized full matches in <10s. All listed invariants hold. No new mocking infrastructure required.
close_reason: Closed

title: Package 13: Season cycle — round-robin → voting → enactment
description: Migration adds seasons table (started_at, ended_at, status: active/voting/enacted/archived). Round-robin generator script seeds full league fixture list at season start: 28 matches per team × 4 conferences, spread over match_cadence_minutes slots. Cup integration: Package 3 already drew brackets — ensure cup R1 fixtures get same kickoff_at discipline so worker picks them up. Season-end detector in worker fires season.ended bus event when all league fixtures status='completed' (SeasonEnactmentListener already exists). 48-hour voting window. Enactment runs existing enactSeasonFocuses() (49 unit tests). Next season seed: roster mutations applied, new fixture list, re-drawn cup brackets.
design: Two cup competitions (Celestial Cup + Solar Shield) advance via the existing CupRoundAdvancerListener — no new code needed there, just verify cup R1 has kickoff_at populated in the seed step.
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end test (with admin fast-forward): seed season → all matches simulate → season.ended fires → voting opens → cast votes → enactment writes mutations → next season seeds with mutated rosters. Existing 49 enactFocus tests still pass.
close_reason: Merged in PR #170 — season lifecycle migration + worker wiring + 22 new tests. Rollover script deferred to isl-qla; migration apply tracked under isl-7lg.

title: Package 10: Match simulation worker (Node)
description: Long-running Node process scripts/match-worker.ts that polls matches WHERE status='scheduled' AND kickoff_at <= now() every 30s, loads teams via getTeamForEngine(), runs simulateFullMatch() (new pure wrapper around gameEngine.genEvent looping minutes 1-90), inserts every event into match_events, updates matches.status='in_progress' + simulated_at, then later marks 'completed' + writes final score + MVP. Settles wagers via existing settlement.ts on completion. Must be unit-testable with injected fake clock + fake Supabase client. Decide deploy target (Railway / Fly / VPS) before merge.
design: Pre-simulate at kickoff in <1s; write all events at once with minute/subminute fields. Clients reveal events via wall-clock elapsed time. No streaming — much simpler than minute-by-minute insertion.
acceptance_criteria: simulateFullMatch() unit tests pass for 100 random matches: scores non-negative, bounded ≤ 12, event count >= 30. Worker integration test: insert a scheduled match with past kickoff_at, run worker tick, assert events persisted + status='completed'. Wager rows transition open → won/lost.
close_reason: Package 10 complete: simulateFullMatch.ts, tests, match-worker.ts, gameEngine.d.ts fix, PR #165

title: Package 9: Match-events schema + match lifecycle
description: Add match_events table (match_id, minute, subminute, type, payload jsonb), extend matches with status enum (scheduled/in_progress/completed/cancelled) + simulated_at, add season_config table (match_cadence_minutes, match_duration_seconds, min_bet). RLS public read on match_events, service-role write. Regenerate database.ts. This is the DB foundation for the playable-state roadmap — Package 10 (worker), 11 (viewer), 13 (season cycle) all depend on it.
design: match_events row-per-event. minute is integer 0-90+; subminute decimal 0-1 for ordering within a minute. payload is jsonb so we don't bloat the schema for every event variant.
acceptance_criteria: Migration applies cleanly. database.ts regenerated. Public can SELECT match_events; only service role can INSERT. Smoke test: insert a few events, verify Realtime postgres_changes broadcasts.
close_reason: Migration 0013 committed at a166a5a. MCP-side apply + database.ts regeneration tracked under isl-du4.

title: sec(M3): updateNotificationPreferences allowlists payload keys (defence-in-depth)
description: src/features/notifications/api/pushSubscriptions.ts:260-294 passes prefs straight into .update(prefs). TypeScript types are erased at runtime — an attacker could pass extra keys like is_admin or credits and the wrapper would forward them. This is exploitable today via the C1 RLS gap; defence-in-depth requires the wrapper to filter keys regardless of what RLS allows. Fix: build a safe object that picks only notify_favourite_team and notify_all_matches before calling .update().
acceptance_criteria: updateNotificationPreferences filters prefs down to the two known boolean keys before the supabase update call. Extra keys in the input (e.g. {is_admin:true}) are dropped silently. Existing UI flows (toggling notify_favourite_team or notify_all_matches) still work; unit test covers the key-filter behaviour.
close_reason: updateNotificationPreferences now builds a fresh allowlist object containing only notify_favourite_team and notify_all_matches (with boolean coercion), then passes it to .update() — extra keys (is_admin, credits, typos) are silently dropped. 5 new tests in pushSubscriptions.test.ts verify the contract; full suite (883 tests) + tsc + eslint all green.

title: sec(M2): service worker client.navigate trusts payload URL without same-origin check
description: public/sw.js:72-84 reads target=event.notification.data.url and calls client.navigate(target) / self.clients.openWindow(target) on whatever URL the encrypted push payload supplied. Today this is safe — only the VAPID-key-holding server can send pushes, and the server hardcodes a same-origin URL. But there is no defence-in-depth check; future code paths that influence the payload would gain a navigation primitive. Fix: parse the URL, refuse cross-origin, fall back to '/' on failure.
acceptance_criteria: Service worker validates that target URL parses as same-origin before navigating; cross-origin/absolute-foreign URLs in the payload result in navigation to '/' or no-op; existing /matches/:id deep-link behaviour unchanged.
close_reason: public/sw.js notificationclick now routes through sameOriginPath() helper that parses the payload URL with new URL(raw, self.location.origin) and returns '/' for any cross-origin / malformed input. client.navigate and self.clients.openWindow both consume the sanitised string. JSDoc + WHY comment documents the threat model.

title: sec(M1): bd-sync-now callerClient uses service-role key, contradicting comments
description: supabase/functions/bd-sync-now/index.ts:189 constructs the caller-identification client with SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY, but the comment block above claims it constructs an 'anon-role client with the caller's JWT' and relies on RLS for safety. In reality RLS is bypassed for that client. Today the only query is filtered by JWT-verified UID and is safe, but any future query added to this code path would silently run as service role — a future-CVE trap. Fix: pass SUPABASE_ANON_KEY for the callerClient. auth.getUser() still resolves the caller via the Authorization header, and RLS will then actually enforce the documented behaviour.
acceptance_criteria: callerClient in bd-sync-now constructed with the anon key. profile lookup still works (returns is_admin for the calling admin user). Tests for the function pass.

title: Relationship Graph 4/6: <RelationshipGraph> SVG component
description:
The actual visible component. Composes the API helpers (1/6), subgraph extractor (2/6), and d3-force layout (3/6) into a drop-in widget for any entity detail page.

Component: src/features/entities/ui/relationshipGraph/RelationshipGraph.tsx
Props: { entityId: string; maxHops?: number; maxNeighbours?: number; className?: string }

Render rules (all using existing tokens from src/components/Layout.tsx:27-40):
- Background: COLORS.abyss; viewport SVG, responsive width, height ~400-500px.
- Nodes: 8px radius circles. Centre/seed node = COLORS.dust filled, 1.5x size, COLORS.quantum ring. Other nodes coloured by entity.kind (mapping documented in a kindColor() helper - player/manager/referee/journalist/etc).
- Edges: lines with stroke-width proportional to |strength|/100 (clamped 0.5-3px). Stroke colour: COLORS.terraNova for strength > 0, COLORS.flare for strength < 0, COLORS.hairline for neutral.
- Labels: monospace tabular, small-caps, only on hover or for the seed node by default.
- Hover: thickens the hovered edge + highlights its two endpoints, dims everything else (opacity 0.35).
- Click on a non-seed node: react-router navigate to /entities/:id (or /players/:id, /managers/:id, /teams/:id based on entity.kind via a route resolver).
- Empty state: centred dust50 text 'NO KNOWN CONNECTIONS' in small-caps when subgraph has zero non-seed nodes.
- Loading state: same small-caps 'PLOTTING CONNECTIONS…' with quantum cursor pulse.

Internal data flow: fetch seed + getEntityRelationships -> buildGraph -> extractSubgraph -> getEntitiesByIds for related nodes -> useForceLayout -> SVG.
acceptance_criteria: Drops into any page given an entityId; renders within 1.5s for graphs of <= 30 nodes; hover/click work with keyboard equivalents; route resolver handles all known entity.kind values; tsc/eslint/vitest green
close_reason: Closed

title: Relationship Graph 3/6: d3-force layout hook
description:
Add the physics simulation that positions nodes for the SVG render layer. Thin React hook wrapping d3-force - the renderer stays our own SVG so the look matches existing design tokens.

Scope:
- Add d3-force (^3.x, ~30KB) to package.json dependencies. Pin minor.
- New hook: src/features/entities/ui/relationshipGraph/useForceLayout.ts
  - Inputs: { nodes: NodeInput[], edges: EdgeInput[], width, height, paused? }
  - Outputs: { nodes: PositionedNode[], edges: PositionedEdge[], restart: () => void }
  - Internally creates a d3-force simulation with forceLink, forceManyBody, forceCenter, forceCollide.
  - Uses requestAnimationFrame to push positions into React state on each tick; stops automatically when simulation cools (alpha < 0.005).
  - Re-runs simulation when input node/edge ids change (not just data shape).
  - Cleans up simulation on unmount.
- Pure logic constants (force strengths, decay rates) co-located in a small forceConfig.ts so they can be tuned without touching the hook.

No tests for the hook itself (d3-force is the trusted dependency); tune visually via the wiring in issue 4/6.
acceptance_criteria: Hook positions a 20-node test graph into a stable layout within ~1.5 seconds; nodes don't overlap (forceCollide working); simulation halts when settled; no setInterval/setTimeout leaks; bundle size delta under 35KB gzipped
close_reason: Closed

title: Relationship Graph 2/6: Subgraph extractor (pure logic)
description:
Pure-logic module that turns a flat relationship list into a bounded N-hop subgraph around a seed entity. No DB, no React, fully unit-testable.

Module to add: src/features/entities/logic/subgraph.ts
- extractSubgraph(graph: RelationshipGraph, seedId: string, opts?: SubgraphOpts): { nodeIds: Set<string>; edges: EntityRelationship[] }
- SubgraphOpts: { maxHops?: number (default 2); maxNeighbours?: number (default 12, prefer highest |strength|); minStrength?: number; kinds?: string[] }
- Reuses existing buildGraph(), neighbours(), neighbourIds() from src/features/entities/logic/relationshipGraph.ts (450 LOC).
- Deterministic ordering when truncating to maxNeighbours (sort by |strength| desc, then id asc).

Test cases: seed with no edges returns just the seed; bidirectional edges deduplicated; maxHops respected; maxNeighbours truncates correctly; minStrength filter works in both directions.
acceptance_criteria: extractSubgraph is pure (no side effects); deterministic for identical inputs; bounded output never exceeds (maxNeighbours per hop) ^ maxHops; co-located test file covers all options + edge cases; tsc/eslint/vitest green
close_reason: Closed

title: Add browser push notifications for match start (1min before kickoff)
description: Users can opt-in to receive browser push notifications 1 minute before matches start. Two opt-in toggles: notify for favourite team's matches, and notify for all matches. Requires service worker, VAPID keys, push_subscriptions table, profile columns, and a new edge function cron worker that dispatches notifications using web-push.
design: Database (migration 0039): push_subscriptions table (one row per device endpoint), profiles.notify_favourite_team + notify_all_matches booleans, match_notification_sends idempotency ledger. Frontend: src/features/notifications/ with checkPushSupport, enablePush, disablePush, NotificationSettings UI on /profile. Service worker at public/sw.js handles push + notificationclick. Edge function supabase/functions/match-notify-worker/ polls every 30s for matches kicking off in 60-90s, joins to opted-in users (favourite team OR all matches), web-pushes them, and records match_notification_sends to dedupe. VAPID keys: VITE_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY (client) + VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY + VAPID_SUBJECT (edge function). Pure logic in vapidKey.ts is unit tested; api layer uses Zod boundary parsing.
notes: Two new env vars needed at deploy: VITE_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY in client build, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY + VAPID_SUBJECT (mailto:) in supabase function secrets. Generate via 'npx web-push generate-vapid-keys'. iOS Safari requires the app to be added to home screen before push will fire; checkPushSupport surfaces a 'no-push-manager' reason for unsupported contexts.

Picks the highest-salience unresolved goals across all agents (e.g. an aging player whose 'retire' goal is reaching threshold; a frustrated manager; a political body with an unfired decree). Calls Sonnet/Opus with full persona + memories context. Output one of: transfer demand, retirement announcement, manager resignation, political decree (e.g. Earth President bans Mars Athletic from trade), feud declaration. Heavily rate-limited — max 2 drama events/day. Writes both a narrative AND structural side effects (player team change, manager swap, season modifier).

close_reason: Phase 9 complete (narrative-only v1). supabase/functions/drama-tick/index.ts: daily cron that picks the SINGLE most-urgent entity (highest summed memory salience in last 14 days, floor 16) and asks Sonnet 4.6 for one dramatic beat. 5 drama narrative kinds: transfer_demand, retirement_announcement, manager_resignation, political_decree, feud_declaration. Steered to entity-kind-appropriate beats (players → transfer/retirement/feud; managers → resignation/feud; political_body → decree). Daily cap MAX_DRAMA_PER_DAY=2 enforced via narratives count. Hallucination guard rejects drafts whose kind isn't in DRAMA_KINDS. Skips entities that already starred in a drama narrative within the URGENCY_WINDOW_DAYS=14 window so the same entity doesn't dominate. Structural side effects (transfer execution, manager swap) DEFERRED — v1 emits narrative only so we can observe drama feel for a week before letting it mutate world state.

Add resolvers shootOrPass.ts (striker decision shaded by rivalry memory with the keeper), cardSeverity.ts (ref booking severity shaded by prior flare-ups with the player). Engine still drives match flow — resolvers only shade probability weights. Hot-path safe: prepareCorpusForMatch() hydrates personas + relevant memories into in-memory map before kickoff. Sync reads only during match. Snapshot test 200-match smoke run for regression.
acceptance_criteria: 200-match smoke test: distribution of cards, shots, goals per team within 5% of pre-refactor baseline. Same player with grudge vs a particular keeper visibly behaves differently across two matches (more aggressive shots). No new DB calls inside the match loop.
close_reason: Phase 8 complete (resolver infrastructure only — engine integration deferred). Two new reflex-tier resolvers under logic/resolvers/: shootOrPass.ts (striker's shoot weight ∈ [0.2,0.8] = neutral 0.5 ± personality + per-keeper memory tally; extraversion raises bold, conscientiousness pulls cautious, scored_on/saw_keeper_falter add, was_saved/missed_target subtract, all clamped) and cardSeverity.ts (ref shades engine's base severity ∈ [0,1] by conscientiousness + per-player flare-ups/goodwill; argued_with_ref/dive_simulated/second_yellow raise, clean_match_with lowers, clamped to [0,1] so cards never fabricated). decisions.ts dispatcher extended with shoot_or_pass + card_severity arms + barrel re-exports. 10 new tests: neutral persona zero-delta, axis-direction, memory direction, clamps, subject-filter (memories about other entities don't contribute). gameEngine.js integration deferred — pure resolvers ship first, observe shape, wire to engine in follow-up.

title: Phase 7: architect-galaxy-tick becomes corpus-first consumer
description: Refactor supabase/functions/architect-galaxy-tick/index.ts: before calling Claude, try composeNarrative()/pickSnippet() against the entity's corpus. Only fall through to LLM on miss. Track miss rate in agent_runs.kind='corpus_hit' vs 'corpus_miss'. Existing rate limits (MAX_ENTITY_NARRATIVES_PER_TICK=3, MAX_ENTITY_POSTS_PER_DAY=1) become enricher-side caps (already in Phase 5).
acceptance_criteria: Galaxy Dispatch keeps publishing at same cadence. agent_runs shows >50% corpus hits after 2 weeks (corpus has had time to fill). LLM cost for galaxy-tick drops measurably.
close_reason: Phase 7 complete. architect-galaxy-tick now runs corpus-first: before each generateEntityNarrative LLM call, queries entity_snippets for the freshest unused snippet (kind in [quote/observation/boast/lament], usage_count < 3, created within 30 days, ordered by usage_count ASC then created_at DESC). HIT path: insert narrative with composed_from pointing at the snippet id, bump snippet usage_count, log 'corpus_hit' to agent_runs with zero tokens. MISS path: log 'corpus_miss' (zero tokens), fall through to existing LLM. Hit/miss rate now queryable from agent_runs.kind = 'corpus_hit' vs 'corpus_miss' for cost reduction observability.

title: Package 11: Live match viewer page
description: src/pages/MatchLive.tsx at route /match/:id. Subscribes to match_events via Supabase Realtime postgres_changes filtered by match_id. Computes elapsedGameMinute = (now - kickoff_at) / match_duration_seconds * 90; renders only events with minute <= elapsedGameMinute. Reuses MatchCard for scoreline + momentum + commentary slots — feed it from realtime data instead of in-browser sim. Late joiners: initial fetch returns all events <= elapsed minute; realtime takes over from there. Handles in-progress, completed, and not-yet-started states.
design: Client-side time reveal (no server push schedule) — keeps the viewer simple and resilient to clock skew. Each new event triggers a CSS fade-in so the feed feels live.
acceptance_criteria: Smoke test: render with frozen kickoff_at, mock realtime, advance time, assert events appear in order. Late-joiner test: render with elapsed=45min, assert only minutes 1-45 visible. Smooth fade-in on new events.

title: sec(L1): tighten bd-sync-now CORS once frontend origin stabilises
description: supabase/functions/bd-sync-now/index.ts:71-75 sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin: '*'. Not directly exploitable today because Supabase JWTs live in localStorage and aren't sent cross-origin by browsers — but tightening to the production origin (https://steverowley.github.io) closes the door on misuse if the auth model ever changes.

title: Relationship Graph 6/6: Polish - a11y, reduced-motion, mobile, perf
description:
Last-mile polish before declaring the relationship graph shippable.

Scope:
- prefers-reduced-motion: skip the d3-force animation; run simulation to completion synchronously (or use a deterministic radial layout fallback) and render a static snapshot.
- Keyboard nav: focusable nodes (tabindex), Enter activates the click handler, arrow keys cycle through neighbours of the focused node. aria-label on each node describes 'NAME, KIND, relationship to seed: KIND, strength: N'.

- Mobile (375 / 768 / 1280): graph viewport shrinks gracefully; touch tap == click; pinch-to-zoom disabled within the graph area (no accidental page zoom); labels surface on tap not just hover.

acceptance_criteria: prefers-reduced-motion respected; keyboard nav works in Firefox + Chrome; aria announcements verified with VoiceOver/NVDA spot check; no jank on mobile Safari at 375px; sim halts on tab hide; tsc/eslint/vitest green
close_reason: Closed

title: Persist all-starter participation for true match history
description:
The /players/:playerId 'Recent Stat Lines' section currently lists only matches where the player recorded a goal, assist, or card. This is because the match-worker (supabase/functions/match-worker/index.ts:407-422) skips inserting match_player_stats rows for players with zero stats. As a result, a defender with 30 clean sheets, a keeper with no logged saves, or an out-of-form striker sees an empty list — which misleadingly reads as 'never played'. We covered this in PR #314 by renaming the section to 'Recent Stat Lines' with an explanatory subtitle and a KNOWN LIMITATION block in src/features/match/api/playerStats.ts, but the underlying gap remains.

Two viable approaches:
  (A) Worker writes all 22 starters with zero-stat rows on match completion. Simpler change but bloats match_player_stats (704 × ~28 matches/season ≈ 20k zero-stat rows per season) and forces the API to filter contributions vs participation downstream.
  (B) New match_lineups table (match_id, player_id, team_id, position, starter, minutes_played) seeded by the worker, with match_player_stats remaining contribution-only. Cleaner separation, but requires a migration and a historical backfill from the engine event log (or accepting that pre-cutover matches stay sparse).

Either approach needs a backfill pass over completed historical matches (currently ~14 seasons of data) and a worker change to start writing the new shape. Once landed, the UI copy on PlayerDetail.tsx ('Recent Stat Lines' + subtitle + 'No stat-line appearances yet' empty state) reverts to 'Recent Matches' / 'No recorded appearances yet', and the KNOWN LIMITATION block in src/features/match/api/playerStats.ts (lines 14-26) is removed.
design: Option B is preferred: dedicated match_lineups table keeps match_player_stats focused on contributions, avoids 20k+ zero rows/season, and gives a cleaner foundation for future surfaces (lineup graphics, formation diagrams, sub histories). Migration adds match_lineups with FKs to matches + players + teams + an enum-ish position column. Worker writes 22 rows per side at kickoff (or one transaction per match on completion). Backfill script reads completed matches and reconstructs lineups from match_events (goalkeeper_init, sub_in, sub_out) plus team starter flags. Existing playerStats.test.ts harness should accommodate a new join with minimal changes.
acceptance_criteria: A defender who played 30 matches with zero goals/assists/cards shows 30 rows in the player detail Recent Matches section. Historical matches are backfilled. PlayerDetail.tsx UI copy reverts to 'Recent Matches' (section title), 'No recorded appearances yet' (empty state), and the subtitle paragraph is removed. The KNOWN LIMITATION block in src/features/match/api/playerStats.ts module header and the IMPORTANT — STAT-ONLY APPEARANCES block in getPlayerRecentMatches JSDoc are removed. All existing playerStats.test.ts cases still pass.
notes: Originating context: PR #314 review thread (Phase C player detail). The KNOWN LIMITATION block in src/features/match/api/playerStats.ts:14-26 and the IMPORTANT section in the getPlayerRecentMatches JSDoc both reference this follow-up. UI copy lives in src/pages/PlayerDetail.tsx around line 281 (section header + subtitle + empty state).
close_reason: Closed

title: Pitch View 5/6: Subtle Architect visual flair v1
description:
Give the Cosmic Architect a visible presence on the pitch when it interferes with events. SUBTLE only - no teleporting players, no reality rewrites yet (those are v2).

Scope:
- Read existing architect* flags already present in event payloads (src/features/match/types.ts:147-162: architectAnnulled, architectForced, architectConjured, architectStolen, architectEcho).
- When an event with any architect flag enters the queue:
  - 600ms COLORS.quantum glow halo (filter: drop-shadow) on the involved player dot.
  - Pitch markings flicker once: opacity 1 -> 0.3 -> 1 over 80ms.
  - Ball briefly trails a COLORS.quantum smear via an SVG line between previous and current ball positions, fading out.
- All flair lives in PitchView.tsx; no engine or DB changes.
- Total flair duration under 700ms so it overlaps the choreography window, doesn't extend it.
- Add a 'fire architect flair' button to PitchDebugOverlay so manual verification doesn't require waiting for a real Architect intervention.
acceptance_criteria: Architect-flagged event triggers halo + flicker + ball trail within 700ms; debug button fires the same effect on demand; no measurable FPS impact during sustained motion; tsc/eslint/vitest green
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 11: Shadow timelines — MiroFish-style ensemble simulations
description: New edge function supabase/functions/shadow-match-worker/index.ts runs 3-5 perturbed copies of each upcoming match in the background using existing simulateFullMatch with different RNG seeds and small persona perturbations. Results stored in new shadow_match_results table — never shown to users directly. Architect council reads shadow distribution before each match to inform interference choices (e.g. 'in 4/5 timelines Earth United wins by >=2 — the Dramatist intervenes').
acceptance_criteria: For every match, 3-5 shadow runs exist in shadow_match_results. Architect council's pre-match decision references shadow distribution in its lore entry. No user-facing change yet (purely informs Architect).
close_reason: Phase 11 complete (cheap monte-carlo v1). Migration 0037_shadow_match_results.sql creates the shadow_match_results table — service-role-only RLS so the alternate-timeline view never leaks to fans. Stores up to N (default 5) shadow outcomes per upcoming match keyed by (match_id, timeline_index). supabase/functions/shadow-match-worker/index.ts: cron-driven (every hour at :15) lightweight monte-carlo that samples outcomes from match_odds + a Poisson goal model — NOT full engine simulation (deferred to v2 since running 4 extra full 90-min sims per fixture would be expensive for marginal signal). Skips matches already at full shadow count via SHADOWS_PER_MATCH = 5. Caps to MATCH_HORIZON = 40 fixtures per tick, SHADOW_HORIZON_HOURS = 36. Goal clamped to MAX_TEAM_GOALS = 6. Architect-council read integration deferred to Phase 12 territory. database.ts hand-mirrored.

Strict perturbation budget so fans can't DOS the simulator.

close_reason: Phase 12 complete (read-only viewer v1). /admin/what-if route shows admins the alternate-timeline distribution from shadow_match_results for upcoming fixtures — empirical home/draw/away counts + scoreline list per match, FIXTURE_LIMIT=20 per page. Mirrors Admin.tsx auth gate (profiles.is_admin); the real boundary is shadow_match_results service-role RLS so a compromised client still can't read shadows. Live perturbation ('what if X is injured') deferred — v1 ships the read-only viewer first, observe its value before building the perturbation engine. Wired into main.tsx as lazy route.

title: Relationship Graph (stretch): Teams as graph nodes
description:
STRETCH / v2 - not part of the initial Relationship Graph ship. Today teams live in the 'teams' table and have NO row in 'entities', so the web-of-connections widget cannot render team-to-team rivalries, parent-club ties, shared owners, or player->team affiliations.

Two implementation paths to evaluate during scoping:

Option A - Shadow entity per team (recommended):
- New migration adds an entities row (kind='team') for every existing teams row, mirrored via trigger on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE of teams.
- entity_relationships then carries team rivalries (kind='derby', 'parent_club', 'shared_owner', etc) naturally.
- TeamDetail.tsx gets the same <RelationshipGraph> drop-in as other detail pages.
- Player -> team edges become first-class (kind='plays_for', strength reflecting tenure or fan-favourite status).

Option B - Parallel team_relationships table:
- Avoids the entity shadow but means the graph component needs branching logic to mix two relationship sources.
- Higher long-term maintenance cost.

Seed data: backfill known rivalries from each league (Mars derbies, Jupiter cluster rivalries, asteroid belt clashes). Hand-curated initial set then let the Architect / lore generators add edges organically.

Do NOT start this until Relationship Graph 1-6 are shipped and proven.
acceptance_criteria: Team-to-team edges render on TeamDetail; player nodes show edges to their team; clicking a team node navigates to /teams/:id; Architect can add/sever team relationships via the existing CosmicArchitect interface (give it a new lever); no regression on existing entity detail graphs
close_reason: Closed

Reuses PitchSurface and the choreographer from earlier issues. Main engineering challenge: avoiding N parallel Realtime subscriptions when many matches are live concurrently. Options to explore during scoping:
- One shared subscription that multiplexes per match_id.
- Lighter-weight 'snapshot' of latest PitchState persisted to matches table, polled at low frequency.

Do NOT start this until Pitch View 1-6 are shipped and stable.
acceptance_criteria: Live matches in /matches show a compact pitch preview with current ball/dot positions; no more than 1 Realtime subscription regardless of how many live matches; no perceptible main-thread jank on the list page

title: Quick-capture floating button
description: Admin-only floating "+" button visible on every page that opens a one-line idea capture modal. Drops the new item into the Ideas column without navigating to /roadmap. Direct fix for the "I had an idea while looking at X" problem.
design: Floating action button bottom-right. Modal is a stripped-down ItemEditorModal (title only). Submit → createItem with status="idea". Keyboard shortcut (cmd-shift-i) opens the modal too.
acceptance_criteria: Button visible on /, /matches, /voting, /training when profile.is_admin. Submitting a title creates a roadmap_items row and the modal closes.
close_reason: Closed
